articles.title: Amara and the Stranger in the Rain
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about persistence, choice, and the stranger in the rain.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Amara is a patient gardener. Amara lives in a dry valley and wants to protect young trees.
One day, a stranger asked for shelter during a storm. Amara has a problem and must decide whether to open the door.

People compare one part of the journey with another. There is a simple reason for this. People often see this in the journey. One problem can change what happens next. Amara is careful before making a choice.
Amara meets an elderly neighbor. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Amara does not stop. The final choice shows persistence.
One clue sits next to another.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

vocabulary.definition: to look at two things to see how they are similar or different
vocabulary.example: Compare the two pictures.
vocabulary.usage: compare A with B 表示比较两者。

vocabulary.definition: a cause or explanation for something
vocabulary.example: There is a reason for the change.
vocabulary.usage: 常用 the reason for 或 the reason why。

vocabulary.definition: many times or regularly
vocabulary.example: We often walk after dinner.
vocabulary.usage: 放在一般动词前、be 动词后。

vocabulary.definition: something difficult that needs an answer
vocabulary.example: We worked together to solve the problem.
vocabulary.usage: 常与 solve, face, cause 搭配。

vocabulary.definition: giving attention so that you avoid mistakes
vocabulary.example: Be careful when you cross the road.
vocabulary.usage: 常用于 be careful 或 careful + 名词。

vocabulary.definition: able to wait calmly
vocabulary.example: Be patient with new words.
vocabulary.usage: patient with sb/sth 表示“对……有耐心”。

vocabulary.definition: a person who grows and cares for plants
vocabulary.example: The gardener waters the flowers.
vocabulary.usage: -er 常表示“做某事的人”。

vocabulary.definition: with little or no water
vocabulary.example: The ground is dry.
vocabulary.usage: 可形容天气、土地或物品。

vocabulary.definition: low land between hills or mountains
vocabulary.example: A river runs through the valley.
vocabulary.usage: 常与 river, hill, mountain 搭配。

phrases.definition: beside or very close to
phrases.example: One clue sits next to another.
phrases.usage: 后面直接接地点或事物。

phrases.definition: across a period, not all at once
phrases.example: Small changes appear over time.
phrases.usage: 常用于描述慢慢发生的变化。

grammar.target: The journey changes over time.
grammar.definition: The present simple describes a fact or repeated process.
grammar.note: 一般现在时用于说明事实、规律或经常发生的动作。
grammar.example: Water freezes at zero degrees.

grammar.target: Amara is a patient gardener.
grammar.definition: The present simple states a fact, habit, or stable situation.
grammar.note: 一般现在时用来说明事实、状态或经常发生的动作。
grammar.example: The town needs clean water.

rhetoric.target: The journey is like a small map.
rhetoric.definition: A simile compares two things using like.
rhetoric.note: 用 like 把主题比作地图，帮助读者形成简单画面。
rhetoric.effect: 把抽象过程变成容易理解的具体物体。
rhetoric.tryIt: A timetable is like a map of the day.

rhetoric.target: They look at the problem and work together.
rhetoric.definition: A concrete detail gives the reader something easy to see or imagine.
rhetoric.note: 用具体细节帮助读者看见场景，而不是只讲抽象道理。
rhetoric.effect: It makes the lesson easier to picture.
rhetoric.tryIt: The small key waits on the table.

articles.title: Elin and the Abandoned Garden
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about responsibility, choice, and the abandoned garden.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Elin is a lighthouse keeper. Elin lives in a rocky coast and wants to guide ships through fog.
One day, an untended garden was drying behind a locked gate. Elin has a problem and must save the plants without damaging the property.
Elin is careful before making a choice. Elin can notice a small change. A clear pattern appears in the journey. The journey changes slowly over time. The result is easy to see.
Elin meets a retired groundskeeper. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Elin does not stop. The final choice shows responsibility.
At first, Elin sees only one part of the journey.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

vocabulary.usage: 常用于 be careful 或 careful + 名词。

vocabulary.definition: to see or become aware of something
vocabulary.example: Did you notice the blue door?
vocabulary.usage: 后面可接名词，或 notice that + 句子。

vocabulary.example: The leaves make a simple pattern.
vocabulary.usage: 常与 see, follow, repeat 搭配。

vocabulary.definition: at a low speed
vocabulary.example: The boat moved slowly.
vocabulary.usage: 通常放在动词之后，说明动作速度。

vocabulary.definition: what happens because of an action
vocabulary.example: The result of the test was clear.
vocabulary.usage: 常见搭配是 the result of 和 as a result。

vocabulary.definition: something difficult that needs an answer
vocabulary.example: We have a small problem.
vocabulary.usage: solve a problem 表示“解决问题”。

vocabulary.definition: an act of choosing between things
vocabulary.example: It was a hard choice.
vocabulary.usage: make a choice 表示“做选择”。

vocabulary.definition: not large
vocabulary.example: A small change can help.
vocabulary.usage: 可修饰具体事物或抽象变化。

vocabulary.definition: an activity that needs effort
vocabulary.example: The plan needs work.
vocabulary.usage: 既可作名词也可作动词。

phrases.usage: 常放在句首，与 later 或 in the end 形成时间对比。

phrases.example: Small changes appear over time.

articles.title: Iris and the Hidden Path
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about compassion, choice, and the hidden path.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Iris is a traveling healer. Iris lives in a snowy mountain region and wants to reach a distant family.
One day, a narrow path appeared after heavy rain. Iris has a problem and must decide whether the unknown route was safe.
People often see this in the journey. One problem can change what happens next. Iris is careful before making a choice. Iris can notice a small change. A clear pattern appears in the journey.
Iris meets a shepherd familiar with the hills. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Iris does not stop. The final choice shows compassion.
The outcome changes because of the surrounding conditions.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

articles.title: Leo and the Silver Contest
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about courage, choice, and the silver contest.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Leo is a shy musician. Leo lives in a hill village and wants to play a song for the festival.
One day, a judge offered a silver prize. Leo has a problem and must complete the task without taking an unfair shortcut.
The result is easy to see. This is a useful part of the journey. People compare one part of the journey with another. There is a simple reason for this. People often see this in the journey.
Leo meets a quiet rival. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Leo does not stop. The final choice shows courage.
The important details appear one by one.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

articles.title: Mina and the Broken Bridge
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about kindness, choice, and the broken bridge.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Mina is a young baker. Mina lives in a busy market town and wants to deliver warm bread.
One day, a night storm broke the wooden bridge. Mina has a problem and must cross the river before sunset.
Mina is careful before making a choice. Mina can notice a small change. A clear pattern appears in the journey. The journey changes slowly over time. The result is easy to see.
Mina meets a patient ferryman. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Mina does not stop. The final choice shows kindness.
At first, Mina sees only one part of the journey.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

articles.title: Niko and the Difficult Promise
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about resourcefulness, choice, and the difficult promise.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Niko is a clever tailor. Niko lives in a crowded city and wants to finish a coat for a traveler.
One day, an easier path appeared after a promise was made. Niko has a problem and must keep the promise despite the delay.
People often see this in the journey. One problem can change what happens next. Niko is careful before making a choice. Niko can notice a small change. A clear pattern appears in the journey.
Niko meets a friend carrying a lantern. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Niko does not stop. The final choice shows resourcefulness.
The outcome changes because of the surrounding conditions.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

articles.title: Pip and the False Rumor
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about cooperation, choice, and the false rumor.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Pip is a small sparrow. Pip lives in an old orchard and wants to build a safe nest.
One day, a frightening story about a neighbor spread through the town. Pip has a problem and must discover what had actually happened.
A clear pattern appears in the journey. The journey changes slowly over time. The result is easy to see. This is a useful part of the journey. People compare one part of the journey with another.
Pip meets a shopkeeper who had seen the event. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Pip does not stop. The final choice shows cooperation.
In the end, Pip understands the journey more clearly.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

articles.title: Rowan and the Lost Purse
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about honesty, choice, and the lost purse.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Rowan is a curious fox. Rowan lives in a green forest and wants to find a hidden spring.
One day, a heavy purse lay beside the road. Rowan has a problem and must find its owner before leaving town.
A clear pattern appears in the journey. The journey changes slowly over time. The result is easy to see. This is a useful part of the journey. People compare one part of the journey with another.
Rowan meets a child selling apples. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Rowan does not stop. The final choice shows honesty.
In the end, Rowan understands the journey more clearly.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

articles.title: Sana and the Shared Well
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about humility, choice, and the shared well.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Sana is a old potter. Sana lives in a riverside village and wants to fire a beautiful bowl.
One day, the only working well began to run low. Sana has a problem and must divide water fairly during a dry week.

People compare one part of the journey with another. There is a simple reason for this. People often see this in the journey. One problem can change what happens next. Sana is careful before making a choice.
Sana meets a farmer with a measuring jar. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Sana does not stop. The final choice shows humility.
One clue sits next to another.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

articles.title: Tomas and the Stopped Clock
articles.levelFocus: very common words, short sentences, and concrete meaning
articles.dek: A graded A1 story about accuracy, choice, and the stopped clock.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Tomas is a mapmaker's apprentice. Tomas lives in a windy harbor and wants to draw an accurate coast.
One day, the town clock stopped before an important meeting. Tomas has a problem and must help people arrive at the right time.
The result is easy to see. This is a useful part of the journey. People compare one part of the journey with another. There is a simple reason for this. People often see this in the journey.
Tomas meets a watchmaker with poor eyesight. They look at the problem and work together.
The work is not easy, but Tomas does not stop. The final choice shows accuracy.
The important details appear one by one.
The journey changes over time.
The journey is like a small map.

articles.title: Amara and the Abandoned Garden
articles.levelFocus: everyday description, sequence, and simple cause and effect
articles.dek: A graded A2 story about persistence, choice, and the abandoned garden.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Amara works as a patient gardener in a dry valley. Amara hopes to protect young trees.
One day, an untended garden was drying behind a locked gate. Because of this, Amara must save the plants without damaging the property.

An ordinary part of the journey can become important. The solution depends on close attention. Amara chooses a different way instead. More than one answer is possible. Amara remains patient when progress is slow.
The first plan fails, so Amara asks a retired groundskeeper for help. Together, they study the problem.
Amara learns to care for a place while respecting who is responsible for it. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Amara understands that persistence is shown through actions.
In this way, the separate details form a complete idea.
When people look closely, they can see the difference.
The journey tells its story quietly.

vocabulary.definition: normal and not unusual
vocabulary.example: It looked like an ordinary box.
vocabulary.usage: 可放在名词前描述日常事物。

vocabulary.definition: an answer to a problem
vocabulary.example: The group found a practical solution.
vocabulary.usage: solution to a problem 是常见搭配。

vocabulary.definition: in place of another action or thing
vocabulary.example: We walked instead of taking the bus.
vocabulary.usage: 可单独使用，也可用 instead of + 名词或 -ing。

vocabulary.definition: able to happen or be done
vocabulary.example: Is it possible to finish today?
vocabulary.usage: it is possible to do 是常见句型。

vocabulary.definition: able to wait without becoming angry
vocabulary.example: Be patient with yourself.
vocabulary.usage: 常见搭配 be patient with + 人或事。

vocabulary.definition: not the same
vocabulary.example: The two answers are different.
vocabulary.usage: be different from ... 表示“与……不同”。

vocabulary.definition: with careful attention
vocabulary.example: Look closely at the picture.
vocabulary.usage: 常修饰 look, watch, listen。

vocabulary.definition: having great value or effect
vocabulary.example: This is an important detail.
vocabulary.usage: important for/to ...。

vocabulary.definition: a description of events, real or imagined
vocabulary.example: The story has a clear ending.
vocabulary.usage: 可指虚构故事或新闻叙述。

phrases.definition: by using this method
phrases.example: In this way, the separate details form a complete idea.
phrases.usage: 用于总结前面的方法及其结果。

phrases.definition: as a result of something
phrases.example: The road closed because of rain.
phrases.usage: 后接名词或名词短语。

grammar.target: When people look closely, they can see the difference.
grammar.definition: A when-clause introduces the condition or time for a general result.
grammar.note: when 从句使用一般现在时，主句用 can 表示通常可能看到的结果。
grammar.example: When the sun appears, we can see the colors clearly.

grammar.target: Because of this, Amara must save the plants without damaging the property.
grammar.definition: Because introduces the reason for an action or result.
grammar.note: because 引导原因从句，说明为什么会发生某事。
grammar.example: The path is closed because the river is high.

rhetoric.target: The journey tells its story quietly.
rhetoric.definition: A non-human thing is given the human action of telling a story.
rhetoric.note: 把主题写成会讲故事的人，使描述更生动。
rhetoric.effect: 暗示细节需要读者主动观察才能被理解。
rhetoric.tryIt: The old house tells its story through its walls.

rhetoric.target: Because of this, Amara must save the plants without damaging the property.
rhetoric.definition: A contrast shows how two choices, ideas, or moments differ.
rhetoric.note: 通过对比突出变化或选择，让文章更有层次。
rhetoric.effect: It helps the reader notice the turning point.
rhetoric.tryIt: The room is quiet, but the decision is loud.

articles.title: Elin and the Stopped Clock
articles.levelFocus: everyday description, sequence, and simple cause and effect
articles.dek: A graded A2 story about responsibility, choice, and the stopped clock.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Elin works as a lighthouse keeper in a rocky coast. Elin hopes to guide ships through fog.
One day, the town clock stopped before an important meeting. Because of this, Elin must help people arrive at the right time.

Elin remains patient when progress is slow. People discover something new about the journey. A small detail helps explain the change. The journey gradually takes a new form. The effect becomes clear after some time.
The first plan fails, so Elin asks a watchmaker with poor eyesight for help. Together, they study the problem.
Elin learns to combine practical knowledge with clear communication. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Elin understands that responsibility is shown through actions.
Elin can find out more by looking closely at the journey.
When people look closely, they can see the difference.
The journey tells its story quietly.

articles.title: Leo and the Difficult Promise
articles.levelFocus: everyday description, sequence, and simple cause and effect
articles.dek: A graded A2 story about courage, choice, and the difficult promise.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Leo works as a shy musician in a hill village. Leo hopes to play a song for the festival.
One day, an easier path appeared after a promise was made. Because of this, Leo must keep the promise despite the delay.

The effect becomes clear after some time. Leo must prepare before the next step. An ordinary part of the journey can become important. The solution depends on close attention. Leo chooses a different way instead.
The first plan fails, so Leo asks a friend carrying a lantern for help. Together, they study the problem.
Leo learns to understand that trust grows through action. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Leo understands that courage is shown through actions.
As soon as one condition changes, another response begins.
When people look closely, they can see the difference.
The journey tells its story quietly.

articles.title: Mina and the Silver Contest
articles.levelFocus: everyday description, sequence, and simple cause and effect
articles.dek: A graded A2 story about kindness, choice, and the silver contest.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Mina works as a young baker in a busy market town. Mina hopes to deliver warm bread.
One day, a judge offered a silver prize. Because of this, Mina must complete the task without taking an unfair shortcut.

Mina remains patient when progress is slow. People discover something new about the journey. A small detail helps explain the change. The journey gradually takes a new form. The effect becomes clear after some time.
The first plan fails, so Mina asks a quiet rival for help. Together, they study the problem.
Mina learns to value honest work more than applause. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Mina understands that kindness is shown through actions.
Mina can find out more by looking closely at the journey.
When people look closely, they can see the difference.
The journey tells its story quietly.

articles.title: Niko and the False Rumor
articles.levelFocus: everyday description, sequence, and simple cause and effect
articles.dek: A graded A2 story about resourcefulness, choice, and the false rumor.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Niko works as a clever tailor in a crowded city. Niko hopes to finish a coat for a traveler.
One day, a frightening story about a neighbor spread through the town. Because of this, Niko must discover what had actually happened.

Niko chooses a different way instead. More than one answer is possible. Niko remains patient when progress is slow. People discover something new about the journey. A small detail helps explain the change.
The first plan fails, so Niko asks a shopkeeper who had seen the event for help. Together, they study the problem.
Niko learns to check evidence before repeating a claim. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Niko understands that resourcefulness is shown through actions.
Niko studies the evidence instead of guessing.
When people look closely, they can see the difference.
The journey tells its story quietly.

articles.title: Pip and the Shared Well
articles.levelFocus: everyday description, sequence, and simple cause and effect
articles.dek: A graded A2 story about cooperation, choice, and the shared well.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Pip works as a small sparrow in an old orchard. Pip hopes to build a safe nest.
One day, the only working well began to run low. Because of this, Pip must divide water fairly during a dry week.

A small detail helps explain the change. The journey gradually takes a new form. The effect becomes clear after some time. Pip must prepare before the next step. An ordinary part of the journey can become important.
The first plan fails, so Pip asks a farmer with a measuring jar for help. Together, they study the problem.
Pip learns to make fairness visible through a rule everyone can understand. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Pip understands that cooperation is shown through actions.
Pip must take care of the smallest details.
When people look closely, they can see the difference.
The journey tells its story quietly.

articles.adaptationNote: An original A2 story written for this reading collection.

One day, a stranger asked for shelter during a storm. Because of this, Rowan must decide whether to open the door.

A small detail helps explain the change. The journey gradually takes a new form. The effect becomes clear after some time. Rowan must prepare before the next step. An ordinary part of the journey can become important.
The first plan fails, so Rowan asks an elderly neighbor for help. Together, they study the problem.
Rowan learns to balance caution with kindness. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Rowan understands that honesty is shown through actions.
Rowan must take care of the smallest details.

articles.levelFocus: everyday description, sequence, and simple cause and effect
articles.dek: A graded A2 story about humility, choice, and the broken bridge.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original A2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Sana works as a old potter in a riverside village. Sana hopes to fire a beautiful bowl.
One day, a night storm broke the wooden bridge. Because of this, Sana must cross the river before sunset.

An ordinary part of the journey can become important. The solution depends on close attention. Sana chooses a different way instead. More than one answer is possible. Sana remains patient when progress is slow.
The first plan fails, so Sana asks a patient ferryman for help. Together, they study the problem.
Sana learns to work with the river rather than fight it. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Sana understands that humility is shown through actions.
In this way, the separate details form a complete idea.
When people look closely, they can see the difference.
The journey tells its story quietly.

body: Tomas works as a mapmaker's apprentice in a windy harbor. Tomas hopes to draw an accurate coast.
One day, a narrow path appeared after heavy rain. Because of this, Tomas must decide whether the unknown route was safe.

The effect becomes clear after some time. Tomas must prepare before the next step. An ordinary part of the journey can become important. The solution depends on close attention. Tomas chooses a different way instead.
The first plan fails, so Tomas asks a shepherd familiar with the hills for help. Together, they study the problem.
Tomas learns to let curiosity ask questions before it takes risks. The slower plan works because it answers the real problem.
At the end of the day, Tomas understands that accuracy is shown through actions.
As soon as one condition changes, another response begins.
When people look closely, they can see the difference.

vocabulary.definition: with careful attention

vocabulary.usage: 常修饰 look, watch, listen。

vocabulary.definition: having great value or effect
vocabulary.example: This is an important detail.
vocabulary.usage: important for/to ...。

vocabulary.definition: a description of events, real or imagined
vocabulary.example: The story has a clear ending.
vocabulary.usage: 可指虚构故事或新闻叙述。

phrases.definition: immediately when
phrases.example: As soon as one condition changes, another response begins.
phrases.usage: 连接两个有先后关系的动作。

phrases.definition: as a result of something
phrases.example: The road closed because of rain.
phrases.usage: 后接名词或名词短语。

grammar.target: When people look closely, they can see the difference.
grammar.definition: A when-clause introduces the condition or time for a general result.
grammar.note: when 从句使用一般现在时，主句用 can 表示通常可能看到的结果。
grammar.example: When the sun appears, we can see the colors clearly.

grammar.target: Because of this, Tomas must decide whether the unknown route was safe.

grammar.note: because 引导原因从句，说明为什么会发生某事。
grammar.example: The path is closed because the river is high.

rhetoric.target: The journey tells its story quietly.

rhetoric.note: 把主题写成会讲故事的人，使描述更生动。
rhetoric.effect: 暗示细节需要读者主动观察才能被理解。
rhetoric.tryIt: The old house tells its story through its walls.

rhetoric.target: Because of this, Tomas must decide whether the unknown route was safe.
rhetoric.definition: A contrast shows how two choices, ideas, or moments differ.
rhetoric.note: 通过对比突出变化或选择，让文章更有层次。
rhetoric.effect: It helps the reader notice the turning point.
rhetoric.tryIt: The room is quiet, but the decision is loud.

articles.title: Amara and the Stopped Clock
articles.levelFocus: connected paragraphs, clear reasons, and familiar abstract ideas
articles.dek: A graded B1 story about persistence, choice, and the stopped clock.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original B1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Amara was a patient gardener who lived in a dry valley. Each morning brought familiar work, but Amara hoped to protect young trees and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, the town clock stopped before an important meeting. The event created a new task: help people arrive at the right time. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Amara alone.

Several conditions influence what happens. A better approach begins with observation. The decision has an unexpected consequence. The method becomes more reliable with repeated checks. A reader can observe a change in the journey.
Amara first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a watchmaker with poor eyesight appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Amara began to understand that combine practical knowledge with clear communication. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Amara felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of persistence.
The system must deal with change over time.
If we ignore the context, we may miss the point.
The change is small, but its meaning is large.

vocabulary.usage: influence + 人/决定/结果，不需要介词 on。

vocabulary.usage: an approach to + 名词或 -ing。

vocabulary.example: Use a reliable source of information.
vocabulary.usage: 常修饰 source, method, person, result。

vocabulary.example: Scientists observe how the material changes.
vocabulary.usage: 可接名词，也可用 observe how/that 引出观察结果。

vocabulary.usage: 常用复数表示整体环境。

vocabulary.definition: more than two but not many
vocabulary.example: Several people joined the work.
vocabulary.usage: 后接复数名词。

vocabulary.definition: happening more than once
vocabulary.example: Repeated practice helps memory.
vocabulary.usage: 由 repeat + -ed 构成。

vocabulary.definition: last in a series
vocabulary.example: The final choice was hard.
vocabulary.usage: final answer/decision。

phrases.usage: 常用于描述慢慢发生的变化。

phrases.definition: used to show a choice or contrast
phrases.example: It invites thought rather than quick judgment.
phrases.usage: 用于对比两个选择。

grammar.target: If we ignore the context, we may miss the point.
grammar.definition: If + present form introduces a possible condition and result.
grammar.note: 条件从句说明可能发生的情况，may 表示结果并非绝对。
grammar.example: If the temperature falls, the water may freeze.

grammar.target: It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed.
grammar.definition: A clause can show contrast, timing, or the condition around the main idea.
grammar.note: 从句可以补充对比、时间或背景，使句子信息更完整。

rhetoric.target: The change is small, but its meaning is large.
rhetoric.definition: Two opposing qualities are placed together for emphasis.
rhetoric.note: small 与 large 形成直接对比。
rhetoric.effect: 突出细微现象可能具有重要意义。

rhetoric.target: It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed.
rhetoric.definition: Cause and effect connects actions with consequences.

rhetoric.effect: It turns a list of events into a meaningful sequence.
rhetoric.tryIt: One missed signal changes the whole plan.

body: Elin was a lighthouse keeper who lived in a rocky coast. Each morning brought familiar work, but Elin hoped to guide ships through fog and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a narrow path appeared after heavy rain. The event created a new task: decide whether the unknown route was safe. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Elin alone.

A reader can observe a change in the journey. The evidence appears in several small details. The journey produces a clear response. The situation develops gradually rather than at once. The final approach proves effective.
Elin first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a shepherd familiar with the hills appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Elin began to understand that let curiosity ask questions before it takes risks. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Elin felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of responsibility.
A full explanation must take into account the conditions around the journey.
If we ignore the context, we may miss the point.
The change is small, but its meaning is large.

Iris first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then an elderly neighbor appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Iris began to understand that balance caution with kindness. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Iris felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of compassion.
A new pattern appears in response to the changing conditions.

body: Leo was a shy musician who lived in a hill village. Each morning brought familiar work, but Leo hoped to play a song for the festival and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a frightening story about a neighbor spread through the town. The event created a new task: discover what had actually happened. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Leo alone.

The final approach proves effective. Understanding the journey can require time. Several conditions influence what happens. A better approach begins with observation. The decision has an unexpected consequence.
Leo first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a shopkeeper who had seen the event appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Leo began to understand that check evidence before repeating a claim. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Leo felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of courage.
As a result, the next stage develops differently.

The change is small, but its meaning is large.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Niko began to understand that make fairness visible through a rule everyone can understand. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Niko felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of resourcefulness.

body: Pip was a small sparrow who lived in an old orchard. Each morning brought familiar work, but Pip hoped to build a safe nest and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.
One day, a night storm broke the wooden bridge. The event created a new task: cross the river before sunset. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Pip alone.

The journey produces a clear response. The situation develops gradually rather than at once. The final approach proves effective. Understanding the journey can require time. Several conditions influence what happens.
Pip first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a patient ferryman appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Pip began to understand that work with the river rather than fight it. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Pip felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of cooperation.
Several conditions play a role in the final result.
If we ignore the context, we may miss the point.

articles.title: Rowan and the Abandoned Garden
articles.levelFocus: connected paragraphs, clear reasons, and familiar abstract ideas
articles.dek: A graded B1 story about honesty, choice, and the abandoned garden.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original B1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Rowan was a curious fox who lived in a green forest. Each morning brought familiar work, but Rowan hoped to find a hidden spring and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, an untended garden was drying behind a locked gate. The event created a new task: save the plants without damaging the property. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Rowan alone.

The journey produces a clear response. The situation develops gradually rather than at once. The final approach proves effective. Understanding the journey can require time. Several conditions influence what happens.
Rowan first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a retired groundskeeper appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Rowan began to understand that care for a place while respecting who is responsible for it. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Rowan felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of honesty.
Several conditions play a role in the final result.
If we ignore the context, we may miss the point.
The change is small, but its meaning is large.

articles.title: Sana and the Silver Contest
articles.levelFocus: connected paragraphs, clear reasons, and familiar abstract ideas
articles.dek: A graded B1 story about humility, choice, and the silver contest.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original B1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Sana was a old potter who lived in a riverside village. Each morning brought familiar work, but Sana hoped to fire a beautiful bowl and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a judge offered a silver prize. The event created a new task: complete the task without taking an unfair shortcut. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Sana alone.

Several conditions influence what happens. A better approach begins with observation. The decision has an unexpected consequence. The method becomes more reliable with repeated checks. A reader can observe a change in the journey.
Sana first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a quiet rival appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Sana began to understand that value honest work more than applause. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Sana felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of humility.
The system must deal with change over time.
If we ignore the context, we may miss the point.
The change is small, but its meaning is large.

articles.title: The Fox and the Crow
articles.levelFocus: clear narrative sequence, motives, and a simple moral
articles.dek: A hungry fox discovers that praise can be a more useful tool than force.
articles.sourceName: Project Gutenberg: Three Hundred Aesop's Fables
articles.adaptationNote: A complete B1 classroom retelling of the public-domain fable, written in modern English.
body: One bright morning, a crow flew over a farm and saw a piece of cheese beside an open window. She dropped quickly, caught the cheese in her beak, and carried it to an old oak tree. There she found a safe perch on a branch high above the road.

A fox was walking below. He had searched for breakfast since sunrise, but the fields had offered him nothing. When he looked up and saw the cheese, his hunger sharpened. He knew that he could not climb the tree, so he decided to use words instead.

"What a magnificent bird!" he called. "Your feathers shine like polished coal. Your wings are strong, and your eyes are bright. Surely a bird as beautiful as you must also have a wonderful voice." The crow had heard warnings about foxes, and for a moment she began to hesitate. Yet she enjoyed the fox's praise and wanted to prove that it was true.

The fox continued to flatter her. "If you truly sing as well as you look, you should be queen of every bird in the forest." The crow could not resist. She lifted her head, opened her beak, and gave a loud caw. At once, the cheese fell to the ground.

The fox caught it before it touched the dust. "Your voice is loud enough," he said, "but wise birds do not believe every compliment." Then he disappeared down the road with his breakfast. The crow remained on the branch, hungry and embarrassed. Her pride had been louder than her caution.

vocabulary.definition: a place where a bird rests, usually above the ground
vocabulary.note: 文中指乌鸦停在树上的落脚处。
vocabulary.example: The parrot returned to its perch.
vocabulary.chinese: 文中指乌鸦停在树上的落脚处。
vocabulary.usage: 结合正文中的语境和上方例句使用，注意词性及常见搭配。

vocabulary.usage: 结合正文中的语境和上方例句使用，注意词性及常见搭配。

vocabulary.note: 乌鸦短暂犹豫，说明她其实意识到了风险。

vocabulary.usage: 结合正文中的语境和上方例句使用，注意词性及常见搭配。

vocabulary.usage: 结合正文中的语境和上方例句使用，注意词性及常见搭配。

vocabulary.usage: 常用于夸赞，语气较强。

vocabulary.definition: to stop yourself from doing something you want to do

vocabulary.usage: cannot resist + 名词/-ing。

grammar.note: 从句可以补充对比、时间或背景，使句子信息更完整。

articles.title: Tomas and the Lost Purse
articles.levelFocus: connected paragraphs, clear reasons, and familiar abstract ideas
articles.dek: A graded B1 story about accuracy, choice, and the lost purse.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original B1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Tomas was a mapmaker's apprentice who lived in a windy harbor. Each morning brought familiar work, but Tomas hoped to draw an accurate coast and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a heavy purse lay beside the road. The event created a new task: find its owner before leaving town. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Tomas alone.

The final approach proves effective. Understanding the journey can require time. Several conditions influence what happens. A better approach begins with observation. The decision has an unexpected consequence.
Tomas first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a child selling apples appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Tomas began to understand that ask careful questions before deciding. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Tomas felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of accuracy.
As a result, the next stage develops differently.
If we ignore the context, we may miss the point.
The change is small, but its meaning is large.

articles.title: Amara and the Hidden Path
articles.levelFocus: detailed explanation, contrast, and implied meaning
articles.dek: A graded B2 story about persistence, choice, and the hidden path.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original B2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Amara was a patient gardener who lived in a dry valley. Each morning brought familiar work, but Amara hoped to protect young trees and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a narrow path appeared after heavy rain. The event created a new task: decide whether the unknown route was safe. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Amara alone.

Several details reinforce the same conclusion. A wider perspective changes how we understand the journey. The wider consequence may not appear immediately. A subtle change can alter the whole experience. A small action can have a significant effect on the journey.
Amara first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a shepherd familiar with the hills appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Amara began to understand that let curiosity ask questions before it takes risks. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Amara felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of persistence.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Amara remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.
To some extent, the result depends on perspective.
Although the first explanation seems simple, the wider system is more complex.
The journey becomes a bridge between separate parts of the explanation.

vocabulary.definition: to make an idea, structure, or feeling stronger
vocabulary.example: Repeated images reinforce the main idea.
vocabulary.usage: reinforce an idea/pattern/message。

vocabulary.definition: a particular way of viewing a situation
vocabulary.example: The decision looks different from her perspective.
vocabulary.usage: from a ... perspective 表示“从某种角度”。

vocabulary.definition: a result that follows from an action or condition
vocabulary.example: The rule had an unintended consequence.
vocabulary.usage: 可用 unintended consequence 表示“意外后果”。

vocabulary.definition: not obvious and therefore difficult to notice
vocabulary.example: There is a subtle difference between the colors.
vocabulary.usage: 常修饰 difference, effect, change, signal。

vocabulary.definition: important or large enough to be noticed
vocabulary.example: The policy produced a significant change.
vocabulary.usage: 常修饰 change, difference, effect。

vocabulary.definition: a statement that makes something clear
vocabulary.example: The explanation connects cause and effect.
vocabulary.usage: explanation of/for ...。

vocabulary.definition: larger in range or influence
vocabulary.example: The issue has a wider effect.
vocabulary.usage: 比较级，用于扩展视角。

vocabulary.definition: not joined or not the same
vocabulary.example: These are separate problems.
vocabulary.usage: 可作形容词或动词。

vocabulary.definition: having many connected parts
vocabulary.example: A complex system needs careful study.
vocabulary.usage: 常修饰 system/problem/process。

phrases.definition: partly but not completely
phrases.example: To some extent, the result depends on perspective.
phrases.usage: 用于限定观点，避免表达过于绝对。

phrases.definition: used to show a choice or contrast
phrases.example: It invites thought rather than quick judgment.
phrases.usage: 用于对比两个选择。

phrases.definition: is decided or changed by something
phrases.example: The answer depends on context.
phrases.usage: 后接名词或 wh- 从句。

grammar.target: Although the first explanation seems simple, the wider system is more complex.
grammar.definition: Although introduces a fact that contrasts with the main clause.
grammar.note: although 引出让步信息，主句给出更重要或相反的判断。
grammar.example: Although the method is fast, it is not always reliable.

rhetoric.target: The journey becomes a bridge between separate parts of the explanation.
rhetoric.definition: The subject is described as a bridge because it connects ideas.
rhetoric.note: 用 bridge 表示主题在多个因素之间建立联系。
rhetoric.effect: 把系统关系转化为读者熟悉的空间形象。
rhetoric.tryIt: Shared language became a bridge between the groups.

rhetoric.target: A wider perspective changes how we understand the journey.
rhetoric.definition: Analytical framing places a detail inside a wider explanation.
rhetoric.note: 把细节放进更大的解释框架中，帮助读者理解复杂关系。
rhetoric.effect: It moves the paragraph from description toward argument.
rhetoric.tryIt: A small choice reveals a wider system.

articles.title: Elin and the Lost Purse
articles.levelFocus: detailed explanation, contrast, and implied meaning
articles.dek: A graded B2 story about responsibility, choice, and the lost purse.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original B2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Elin was a lighthouse keeper who lived in a rocky coast. Each morning brought familiar work, but Elin hoped to guide ships through fog and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a heavy purse lay beside the road. The event created a new task: find its owner before leaving town. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Elin alone.

A small action can have a significant effect on the journey. People interpret the same detail in different ways. One assumption shapes the first explanation. Ultimately, the outcome depends on several connected choices. Elin must adapt when the conditions change.
Elin first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a child selling apples appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Elin began to understand that ask careful questions before deciding. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Elin felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of responsibility.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Elin remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.
In contrast, a wider view reveals several connected causes.
Although the first explanation seems simple, the wider system is more complex.
The journey becomes a bridge between separate parts of the explanation.

One day, an untended garden was drying behind a locked gate. The event created a new task: save the plants without damaging the property. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Iris alone.

The wider consequence may not appear immediately. A subtle change can alter the whole experience. A small action can have a significant effect on the journey. People interpret the same detail in different ways. One assumption shapes the first explanation.
Iris first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a retired groundskeeper appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Iris began to understand that care for a place while respecting who is responsible for it. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Iris felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of compassion.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Iris remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.
In the long run, repeated choices reshape the outcome.

body: Leo was a shy musician who lived in a hill village. Each morning brought familiar work, but Leo hoped to play a song for the festival and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, the only working well began to run low. The event created a new task: divide water fairly during a dry week. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Leo alone.

Leo must adapt when the conditions change. Every solution must work within a constraint. Several details reinforce the same conclusion. A wider perspective changes how we understand the journey. The wider consequence may not appear immediately.
Leo first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a farmer with a measuring jar appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Leo began to understand that make fairness visible through a rule everyone can understand. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Leo felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of courage.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Leo remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.
The contrast can draw attention to a hidden detail.
Although the first explanation seems simple, the wider system is more complex.
The journey becomes a bridge between separate parts of the explanation.

Niko first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a patient ferryman appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Niko began to understand that work with the river rather than fight it. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Niko felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of resourcefulness.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Niko remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.

body: Pip was a small sparrow who lived in an old orchard. Each morning brought familiar work, but Pip hoped to build a safe nest and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a judge offered a silver prize. The event created a new task: complete the task without taking an unfair shortcut. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Pip alone.

One assumption shapes the first explanation. Ultimately, the outcome depends on several connected choices. Pip must adapt when the conditions change. Every solution must work within a constraint. Several details reinforce the same conclusion.
Pip first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a quiet rival appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Pip began to understand that value honest work more than applause. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Pip felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of cooperation.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Pip remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.
Small differences can give rise to a larger effect.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Rowan began to understand that combine practical knowledge with clear communication. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Rowan felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of honesty.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Rowan remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.

articles.adaptationNote: An original B2 story written for this reading collection.
body: Sana was a old potter who lived in a riverside village. Each morning brought familiar work, but Sana hoped to fire a beautiful bowl and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, an easier path appeared after a promise was made. The event created a new task: keep the promise despite the delay. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Sana alone.

Several details reinforce the same conclusion. A wider perspective changes how we understand the journey. The wider consequence may not appear immediately. A subtle change can alter the whole experience. A small action can have a significant effect on the journey.
Sana first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a friend carrying a lantern appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Sana began to understand that understand that trust grows through action. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Sana felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of humility.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Sana remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.
To some extent, the result depends on perspective.
Although the first explanation seems simple, the wider system is more complex.
The journey becomes a bridge between separate parts of the explanation.

articles.title: The Emperor's New Clothes
articles.levelFocus: social pressure, irony, and inferred meaning
articles.dek: An emperor's love of fashion becomes a test of who is willing to tell the truth.
articles.sourceName: Project Gutenberg: Andersen's Fairy Tales
articles.adaptationNote: A complete B2 classroom retelling of Hans Christian Andersen's public-domain tale.
body: There once lived an emperor who cared more about clothes than about laws, roads, or the lives of his people. He changed his coat several times a day and spent hours deciding which colors made him appear most powerful. Visitors did not say, "The emperor is in council." They said, "The emperor is in his dressing room."

Two dishonest weavers arrived in the capital and heard about his weakness. They claimed that they could make a magnificent cloth with a magical quality: it would be invisible to anyone who was foolish or unfit for an important job. The emperor immediately imagined a splendid suit. It would make him look remarkable, and it would also reveal which of his officials were not competent.

The weavers asked for fine silk and gold thread. Then they hid these materials and worked at empty looms. A few days later, the emperor sent his oldest minister to inspect the cloth. The minister stared at the bare wooden frame. He could see nothing, but he was afraid to admit it. If he told the truth, everyone might think he was a fool.

"The pattern is extraordinary," the minister said. "The colors are unlike anything I have seen." Other officials visited and gave the same report. Each person feared the judgment of the others, so each person went along with the lie. The more they praised the cloth, the more difficult honesty became.

At last, the weavers announced that the suit was ready. They pretended to dress the emperor, lifting invisible sleeves and fastening invisible buttons. The emperor saw only his own skin in the mirror, yet he also remained silent. He would rather appear ridiculous than appear incompetent.

The emperor entered a public procession. Crowds filled the streets, and everyone had heard about the magical cloth. People praised the empty sleeves and the long, invisible train. Then a child called out, "But he is wearing nothing at all!" A whisper moved through the crowd and grew into laughter.

The emperor understood that the child was right. Still, he raised his head and continued walking while his servants pretended to carry the train behind him. The finest clothes in the kingdom had been made of fear, and everyone had helped to sew them.

vocabulary.definition: extremely beautiful, impressive, or important
vocabulary.note: 骗子用夸张的形容词吸引皇帝。
vocabulary.example: The hall has a magnificent painted ceiling.
vocabulary.chinese: 骗子用夸张的形容词吸引皇帝。
vocabulary.usage: 结合正文中的语境和上方例句使用，注意词性及常见搭配。

vocabulary.definition: having enough skill or ability to do something well
vocabulary.note: 皇帝想借新衣判断官员是否称职。
vocabulary.example: She is a highly competent engineer.
vocabulary.chinese: 皇帝想借新衣判断官员是否称职。
vocabulary.usage: 结合正文中的语境和上方例句使用，注意词性及常见搭配。

vocabulary.example: A wedding procession crossed the square.

articles.title: Tomas and the Stranger in the Rain

articles.dek: A graded B2 story about accuracy, choice, and the stranger in the rain.

body: Tomas was a mapmaker's apprentice who lived in a windy harbor. Each morning brought familiar work, but Tomas hoped to draw an accurate coast and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a stranger asked for shelter during a storm. The event created a new task: decide whether to open the door. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Tomas alone.

Tomas must adapt when the conditions change. Every solution must work within a constraint. Several details reinforce the same conclusion. A wider perspective changes how we understand the journey. The wider consequence may not appear immediately.
Tomas first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then an elderly neighbor appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Tomas began to understand that balance caution with kindness. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Tomas felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of accuracy.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Tomas remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.
The contrast can draw attention to a hidden detail.
Although the first explanation seems simple, the wider system is more complex.
The journey becomes a bridge between separate parts of the explanation.

articles.title: Amara and the Lost Purse
articles.levelFocus: nuanced argument, flexible syntax, and precise abstract language
articles.dek: A graded C1 story about persistence, choice, and the lost purse.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original C1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Amara was a patient gardener who lived in a dry valley. Each morning brought familiar work, but Amara hoped to protect young trees and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a heavy purse lay beside the road. The event created a new task: find its owner before leaving town. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Amara alone.

The outcome is contingent on conditions that may change. A strong solution must reconcile competing needs. An underlying pattern connects the visible details. The final result may seem counterintuitive at first. A closer reading reveals a further nuance.
Amara first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a child selling apples appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Amara began to understand that ask careful questions before deciding. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Amara felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of persistence.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Amara remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.

Years later, the story was still told in a dry valley. Its lesson was not that every obstacle has a perfect answer. It was that character becomes visible when convenience, fear, and responsibility point in different directions.
The new evidence may call into question the first explanation.
What appears to be a small detail can shape the whole experience.
Beneath the visible surface, the journey keeps a second history.

vocabulary.definition: dependent on particular conditions
vocabulary.example: Success is contingent on public trust.
vocabulary.usage: be contingent on/upon 是固定搭配。

vocabulary.definition: to find a way for opposing needs or ideas to exist together
vocabulary.example: The plan must reconcile speed with safety.
vocabulary.usage: reconcile A with B 表示调和两者。

vocabulary.definition: important but not immediately visible
vocabulary.example: The debate revealed an underlying conflict.
vocabulary.usage: 常修饰 cause, assumption, pattern, principle。

vocabulary.definition: opposite to what people naturally expect
vocabulary.example: The research produced a counterintuitive result.
vocabulary.usage: 常用于 a counterintuitive result/finding。

vocabulary.definition: a slight but important difference in meaning or feeling
vocabulary.example: The translation loses an important nuance.
vocabulary.usage: 常用 capture/lose a nuance 或 a nuance of。

vocabulary.definition: able to be seen or noticed
vocabulary.example: The visible result hides deeper causes.
vocabulary.usage: 常与 invisible 形成对比。

vocabulary.definition: past events connected with a person, place, or thing
vocabulary.example: The object carries a history.
vocabulary.usage: 可指宏大历史，也可指事物经历。

vocabulary.definition: knowledge or feeling gained from events
vocabulary.example: Experience shapes judgment.
vocabulary.usage: 可数表示经历，不可数表示经验。

vocabulary.definition: facts or signs used to support a judgment
vocabulary.example: Evidence may be incomplete.
vocabulary.usage: 学术写作高频词。

phrases.definition: to create doubt about something
phrases.example: The new evidence may call into question the first explanation.
phrases.usage: 常用于证据动摇旧观点。

phrases.definition: in the beginning
phrases.example: At first, the idea seems simple.
phrases.usage: 常与 later 形成时间推进。

phrases.definition: used to show a choice or contrast
phrases.example: It invites thought rather than quick judgment.
phrases.usage: 用于对比两个选择。

grammar.target: What appears to be a small detail can shape the whole experience.
grammar.definition: A what-clause turns a complete idea into the subject of the sentence.
grammar.note: what 从句整体作主语，适合先呈现表象，再提出更复杂的判断。
grammar.example: What seems like a delay may prevent a larger failure.

grammar.target: The outcome is contingent on conditions that may change.
grammar.definition: Advanced sentences often qualify a claim by adding context, contrast, or limitation.
grammar.note: 高阶句子常用从句或限定结构补充语境、对比或限制，让观点更精确。
grammar.example: While the surface story is simple, the deeper issue is contingent on context.

rhetoric.target: Beneath the visible surface, the journey keeps a second history.
rhetoric.definition: The subject is imagined as containing a hidden record below its surface.
rhetoric.note: surface 与 second history 共同表现可见现象背后的累积过程。
rhetoric.effect: 为复杂分析提供一个统一且可记忆的意象。
rhetoric.tryIt: Beneath the polished result, the project keeps a history of compromise.

rhetoric.target: The outcome is contingent on conditions that may change.
rhetoric.definition: Layered interpretation asks the reader to look beyond the obvious surface meaning.
rhetoric.note: 分层解读把表层现象和深层结构连接起来，适合 C1 级别的批判阅读。
rhetoric.effect: It creates depth and invites reflection.
rhetoric.tryIt: Beneath the policy, a question of trust remains.

articles.title: Elin and the Stranger in the Rain

body: Elin was a lighthouse keeper who lived in a rocky coast. Each morning brought familiar work, but Elin hoped to guide ships through fog and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a stranger asked for shelter during a storm. The event created a new task: decide whether to open the door. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Elin alone.

A closer reading reveals a further nuance. We need to scrutinize the conditions surrounding the journey. The first signal remains ambiguous. These minor choices matter cumulatively. The broader implication reaches beyond the immediate example.
Elin first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then an elderly neighbor appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Elin began to understand that balance caution with kindness. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Elin felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of responsibility.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Elin remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.

Years later, the story was still told in a rocky coast. Its lesson was not that every obstacle has a perfect answer. It was that character becomes visible when convenience, fear, and responsibility point in different directions.
A quick solution may work at the expense of a less visible need.
What appears to be a small detail can shape the whole experience.
Beneath the visible surface, the journey keeps a second history.

articles.title: Iris and the Stopped Clock
articles.levelFocus: nuanced argument, flexible syntax, and precise abstract language
articles.dek: A graded C1 story about compassion, choice, and the stopped clock.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original C1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Iris was a traveling healer who lived in a snowy mountain region. Each morning brought familiar work, but Iris hoped to reach a distant family and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, the town clock stopped before an important meeting. The event created a new task: help people arrive at the right time. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Iris alone.

An underlying pattern connects the visible details. The final result may seem counterintuitive at first. A closer reading reveals a further nuance. We need to scrutinize the conditions surrounding the journey. The first signal remains ambiguous.
Iris first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a watchmaker with poor eyesight appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Iris began to understand that combine practical knowledge with clear communication. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Iris felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of compassion.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Iris remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.

Years later, the story was still told in a snowy mountain region. Its lesson was not that every obstacle has a perfect answer. It was that character becomes visible when convenience, fear, and responsibility point in different directions.
In light of the wider context, the simple answer becomes less convincing.
What appears to be a small detail can shape the whole experience.
Beneath the visible surface, the journey keeps a second history.

articles.title: Leo and the Broken Bridge
articles.levelFocus: nuanced argument, flexible syntax, and precise abstract language
articles.dek: A graded C1 story about courage, choice, and the broken bridge.
articles.sourceName: English Reading Lab original story collection
articles.adaptationNote: An original C1 story written for this reading collection.
body: Leo was a shy musician who lived in a hill village. Each morning brought familiar work, but Leo hoped to play a song for the festival and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.
One day, a night storm broke the wooden bridge. The event created a new task: cross the river before sunset. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Leo alone.

The broader implication reaches beyond the immediate example. Context can mediate the effect of the journey. The outcome is contingent on conditions that may change. A strong solution must reconcile competing needs. An underlying pattern connects the visible details.
Leo first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a patient ferryman appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Leo began to understand that work with the river rather than fight it. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Leo felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of courage.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Leo remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.

Years later, the story was still told in a hill village. Its lesson was not that every obstacle has a perfect answer. It was that character becomes visible when convenience, fear, and responsibility point in different directions.
The outcome may be contingent on conditions that remain uncertain.
What appears to be a small detail can shape the whole experience.
Beneath the visible surface, the journey keeps a second history.

body: Niko was a clever tailor who lived in a crowded city. Each morning brought familiar work, but Niko hoped to finish a coat for a traveler and become worthy of the trust that others had placed nearby.

One day, a judge offered a silver prize. The event created a new task: complete the task without taking an unfair shortcut. Turning back would have been easy, yet the choice would have affected more than Niko alone.

An underlying pattern connects the visible details. The final result may seem counterintuitive at first. A closer reading reveals a further nuance. We need to scrutinize the conditions surrounding the journey. The first signal remains ambiguous.
Niko first tried the quickest answer. It failed because the situation demanded attention rather than speed. Then a quiet rival appeared and asked what had already been tried.

Together they studied the place, the available tools, and the time that remained. Niko began to understand that value honest work more than applause. The next attempt was slower, but every step answered a real part of the problem.

Before the task was complete, another difficulty appeared. Niko felt the pull of an easier choice and remembered the original goal. That moment, rather than the final success, became the true test of resourcefulness.

By evening, the work was finished. The people who had been waiting saw only the result, while Niko remembered the uncertainty behind it. Praise mattered less than the knowledge that the decision could be explained honestly.

Years later, the story was still told in a crowded city. Its lesson was not that every obstacle has a perfect answer. It was that character becomes visible when convenience, fear, and responsibility point in different directions.
In light of the wider context, the simple answer becomes less convincing.
What appears to be a small detail can shape the whole experience.
